name: Workers' Comp for Cancer from Chemicals in Taylorsville

mainEntity.name: Can I claim workers' comp for cancer from workplace chemicals in Taylorsville?
mainEntity.text: Can I claim workers' comp for cancer from workplace chemicals in Taylorsville?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, if medical evidence links cancer (e.g., benzene leukemia) to Taylorsville workplace exposure at 'more probable than not' level.

suggestedAnswer.text: Latent cancers need exposure timelines matching latency periods. Taylorsville manufacturing claims require oncologist nexus letters. Insurers blame smoking—counter with studies. Utah pays for chemo/treatment; permanent total if terminal. File early despite diagnosis delay.

description: Yes, if medical evidence links cancer (e.g., benzene leukemia) to Taylorsville workplace exposure at 'more probable than not' level.
